title: Go Checksum
documentation:
## Purpose
Carries a `go.sum` file that records the cryptographic hashes of all module zip archives and `go.mod` files that a Go module depends on, either directly or transitively. The Go toolchain verifies these hashes on every build to detect tampering or supply chain corruption.

## When to use
Use `media:gosum;textable;list` when a pipeline node audits, regenerates, or validates Go dependency checksums — for example, a security scan step that cross-references hashes against a transparency log, or a CI step verifying that `go.sum` is consistent with `go.mod`.

## Notes
The `go.sum` file is maintained automatically by `go mod tidy` and related commands. It should be committed to version control alongside [Go Module](https://capdag.com/browse/media:gomod;textable) (`go.mod`). Removing or editing entries manually can break builds. See also [Go Checksum](https://capdag.com/browse/media:gosum;textable;list).
